title: Map computeIfAbsent() and computeIfPresent() should not be used to add null values
description: This rule ensures that the Java Map methods computeIfAbsent() and computeIfPresent() are not used to add null values to a map. Adding null values can lead to NullPointerExceptions and unexpected behavior when retrieving values from the map.
recommendation: Avoid returning null from the mapping functions passed to computeIfAbsent() and computeIfPresent(). Always return a non-null value or remove the mapping if no value is to be associated.
impact: Adding null values to a map using computeIfAbsent() or computeIfPresent() can cause NullPointerExceptions and make the code less robust.

compliant: map.computeIfAbsent(key, k -> new Value()); // returns a non-null value
non_compliant: map.computeIfAbsent(key, k -> null); // returns null, which is not allowed

checks.message: Avoid returning null from the mapping functions passed to computeIfAbsent() and computeIfPresent(). Always return a non-null value or remove the mapping if no value is to be associated.
